title: Sporobolus glaucifolius
taxonomy: Sporobolus glaucifolius is classified in the family Poaceae and is commonly known as the blue grass. It belongs to the order Poales, which consists of monocotyledonous flowering plants. The genus Sporobolus is a group of grasses, and S. glaucifolius is one of its species. This classification places it within the larger group of grasses, which are widespread across the globe. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group, containing many species of grasses.
uses: Sporobolus glaucifolius, a type of grass native to Australia, has been traditionally used in medicine by the Indigenous Australians.
